title: Mātaṅgī Sādhana
subcategory: Mahāvidyā (tenth) — Ucchiṣṭa-Caṇḍālinī / goddess of leftover & transgressive offerings
century: Medieval (c. 9th–14th c.); digest literature c. 16th–19th c.
description: The tenth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Mātaṅgī (also "Ucchiṣṭa-Caṇḍālinī", "Rāja-Mātaṅgī", "Śyāmā") is the tantric form of Sarasvatī — a green-dark-complexioned goddess offered leftover food (*ucchiṣṭa*) from stained hands, the goddess of speech, music, and the periphery. High caution — an antinomian Mahāvidyā rite whose core offering frame deliberately violates Brahminical purity codes. Preserved here
